segments.text: O God, whom I praise, do not remain silent,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: for people who are wicked and deceitful have opened their mouths against me; they speak against me with lying tongues.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: of the deceitful: Heb. of deceit
are opened: Heb. have opened themselves

segments.text: They surround me with hateful words and attack me without cause.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In return for my friendship they accuse me, but I am a man of prayer.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They repay me evil for good, and hatred for my friendship.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Appoint someone evil to oppose my enemy; let an accuser stand at his right hand.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When he is tried, let him be found guilty, and may his prayers condemn him.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: be condemned: Heb. go out guilty, or, wicked

segments.text: May his days be few; may another take his office.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May his children be fatherless and his wife a widow.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May his children be wandering beggars; may they be driven from their ruined homes.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May a creditor seize all he has; may strangers plunder the fruits of his labor.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May no one extend kindness to him or take pity on his fatherless children.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May his descendants be cut off, their names blotted out from the next generation.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the Lord; may the sin of his mother never be blotted out.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May their sins always remain before the Lord, that he may blot out their name from the earth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For he never thought of doing a kindness, but hounded to death the poor and the needy and the brokenhearted.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He loved to pronounce a curse—may it come back on him. He found no pleasure in blessing—may it be far from him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He wore cursing as his garment; it entered into his body like water, like oil into his bones.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: into his bowels: Heb. within him

segments.text: May it be like a cloak wrapped about him, like a belt tied forever around him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May this be the Lord's payment to my accusers, to those who speak evil against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But you, Sovereign Lord, deal well with me for your name's sake; out of the goodness of your love, deliver me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For I am poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I fade away like an evening shadow; I am shaken off like a locust.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My knees are weak from fasting; my body is thin and gaunt.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am an object of scorn to my accusers; when they see me, they shake their heads.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Help me, Lord my God; save me in accordance with your love.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them know that it is your hand, that you, Lord, have done it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: While they curse, may you bless; may those who attack me be put to shame, but may your servant rejoice.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May my accusers be clothed with disgrace and wrapped in shame as in a cloak.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: With my mouth I will greatly extol the Lord; in the great throng of worshipers I will praise him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For he stands at the right hand of the needy, to save their lives from those who would condemn them.
segments.speaker: David
